<source_slice path="lib/compress/zstd_compress_internal.h" lines="1310-1337">
1310: /* Short Cache */
1311: 
1312: /* Normally, zstd matchfinders follow this flow:
1313:  *     1. Compute hash at ip
1314:  *     2. Load index from hashTable[hash]
1315:  *     3. Check if *ip == *(base + index)
1316:  * In dictionary compression, loading *(base + index) is often an L2 or even L3 miss.
1317:  *
1318:  * Short cache is an optimization which allows us to avoid step 3 most of the time
1319:  * when the data doesn't actually match. With short cache, the flow becomes:
1320:  *     1. Compute (hash, currentTag) at ip. currentTag is an 8-bit independent hash at ip.
1321:  *     2. Load (index, matchTag) from hashTable[hash]. See ZSTD_writeTaggedIndex to understand how this works.
1322:  *     3. Only if currentTag == matchTag, check *ip == *(base + index). Otherwise, continue.
1323:  *
1324:  * Currently, short cache is only implemented in CDict hashtables. Thus, its use is limited to
1325:  * dictMatchState matchfinders.
1326:  */
1327: #define ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_BITS 8
1328: #define ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_MASK ((1u << ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_BITS) - 1)
1329: 
1330: /* Helper function for ZSTD_fillHashTable and ZSTD_fillDoubleHashTable.
1331:  * Unpacks hashAndTag into (hash, tag), then packs (index, tag) into hashTable[hash]. */
1332: MEM_STATIC void ZSTD_writeTaggedIndex(U32* const hashTable, size_t hashAndTag, U32 index) {
1333:     size_t const hash = hashAndTag >> ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_BITS;
1334:     U32 const tag = (U32)(hashAndTag & ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_MASK);
1335:     assert(index >> (32 - ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_BITS) == 0);
1336:     hashTable[hash] = (index << ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_BITS) | tag;
1337: }
</source_slice>

234: /* Returns row matchfinder usage given an initial mode and cParams */
235: static ZSTD_paramSwitch_e ZSTD_resolveRowMatchFinderMode(ZSTD_paramSwitch_e mode,
236:                                                          const ZSTD_compressionParameters* const cParams) {
237: #if defined(ZSTD_ARCH_X86_SSE2) || defined(ZSTD_ARCH_ARM_NEON)
238:     int const kHasSIMD128 = 1;
239: #else
240:     int const kHasSIMD128 = 0;
241: #endif
242:     if (mode != ZSTD_ps_auto) return mode; /* if requested enabled, but no SIMD, we still will use row matchfinder */
243:     mode = ZSTD_ps_disable;
244:     if (!ZSTD_rowMatchFinderSupported(cParams->strategy)) return mode;
245:     if (kHasSIMD128) {
246:         if (cParams->windowLog > 14) mode = ZSTD_ps_enable;
247:     } else {
248:         if (cParams->windowLog > 17) mode = ZSTD_ps_enable;
249:     }
250:     return mode;
251: }

783: /*-*************************************
784:  *  Hashes
785:  ***************************************/
786: static const U32 prime3bytes = 506832829U;

<source_slice path="lib/compress/zstd_compress_internal.h" lines="1318-1337">
1318:  * Short cache is an optimization which allows us to avoid step 3 most of the time
1319:  * when the data doesn't actually match. With short cache, the flow becomes:
1320:  *     1. Compute (hash, currentTag) at ip. currentTag is an 8-bit independent hash at ip.
1321:  *     2. Load (index, matchTag) from hashTable[hash]. See ZSTD_writeTaggedIndex to understand how this works.
1322:  *     3. Only if currentTag == matchTag, check *ip == *(base + index). Otherwise, continue.
1323:  *
1324:  * Currently, short cache is only implemented in CDict hashtables. Thus, its use is limited to
1325:  * dictMatchState matchfinders.
1326:  */
1327: #define ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_BITS 8
1328: #define ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_MASK ((1u << ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_BITS) - 1)
1329: 
1330: /* Helper function for ZSTD_fillHashTable and ZSTD_fillDoubleHashTable.
1331:  * Unpacks hashAndTag into (hash, tag), then packs (index, tag) into hashTable[hash]. */
1332: MEM_STATIC void ZSTD_writeTaggedIndex(U32* const hashTable, size_t hashAndTag, U32 index) {
1333:     size_t const hash = hashAndTag >> ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_BITS;
1334:     U32 const tag = (U32)(hashAndTag & ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_MASK);
1335:     assert(index >> (32 - ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_BITS) == 0);
1336:     hashTable[hash] = (index << ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_BITS) | tag;
1337: }
</source_slice>

<source_slice path="lib/compress/zstd_compress_internal.h" lines="1310-1336">
1310: /* Short Cache */
1311: 
1312: /* Normally, zstd matchfinders follow this flow:
1313:  *     1. Compute hash at ip
1314:  *     2. Load index from hashTable[hash]
1315:  *     3. Check if *ip == *(base + index)
1316:  * In dictionary compression, loading *(base + index) is often an L2 or even L3 miss.
1317:  *
1318:  * Short cache is an optimization which allows us to avoid step 3 most of the time
1319:  * when the data doesn't actually match. With short cache, the flow becomes:
1320:  *     1. Compute (hash, currentTag) at ip. currentTag is an 8-bit independent hash at ip.
1321:  *     2. Load (index, matchTag) from hashTable[hash]. See ZSTD_writeTaggedIndex to understand how this works.
1322:  *     3. Only if currentTag == matchTag, check *ip == *(base + index). Otherwise, continue.
1323:  *
1324:  * Currently, short cache is only implemented in CDict hashtables. Thus, its use is limited to
1325:  * dictMatchState matchfinders.
1326:  */
1327: #define ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_BITS 8
1328: #define ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_MASK ((1u << ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_BITS) - 1)
1329: 
1330: /* Helper function for ZSTD_fillHashTable and ZSTD_fillDoubleHashTable.
1331:  * Unpacks hashAndTag into (hash, tag), then packs (index, tag) into hashTable[hash]. */
1332: MEM_STATIC void ZSTD_writeTaggedIndex(U32* const hashTable, size_t hashAndTag, U32 index) {
1333:     size_t const hash = hashAndTag >> ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_BITS;
1334:     U32 const tag = (U32)(hashAndTag & ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_MASK);
1335:     assert(index >> (32 - ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_BITS) == 0);
1336:     hashTable[hash] = (index << ZSTD_SHORT_CACHE_TAG_BITS) | tag;
</source_slice>

1404: /** ZSTD_adjustCParams_internal() :
1405:  *  optimize `cPar` for a specified input (`srcSize` and `dictSize`).
1406:  *  mostly downsize to reduce memory consumption and initialization latency.
1407:  * `srcSize` can be ZSTD_CONTENTSIZE_UNKNOWN when not known.
1408:  * `mode` is the mode for parameter adjustment. See docs for `ZSTD_cParamMode_e`.

219: const seqStore_t* ZSTD_getSeqStore(const ZSTD_CCtx* ctx) { return &(ctx->seqStore); }

221: /* Returns true if the strategy supports using a row based matchfinder */
222: static int ZSTD_rowMatchFinderSupported(const ZSTD_strategy strategy) {
223:     return (strategy >= ZSTD_greedy && strategy <= ZSTD_lazy2);
224: }
225: 
226: /* Returns true if the strategy and useRowMatchFinder mode indicate that we will use the row based matchfinder
227:  * for this compression.
228:  */
229: static int ZSTD_rowMatchFinderUsed(const ZSTD_strategy strategy, const ZSTD_paramSwitch_e mode) {
230:     assert(mode != ZSTD_ps_auto);
231:     return ZSTD_rowMatchFinderSupported(strategy) && (mode == ZSTD_ps_enable);
232: }
233: 
234: /* Returns row matchfinder usage given an initial mode and cParams */
235: static ZSTD_paramSwitch_e ZSTD_resolveRowMatchFinderMode(ZSTD_paramSwitch_e mode,
236:                                                          const ZSTD_compressionParameters* const cParams) {
237: #if defined(ZSTD_ARCH_X86_SSE2) || defined(ZSTD_ARCH_ARM_NEON)
238:     int const kHasSIMD128 = 1;
239: #else
240:     int const kHasSIMD128 = 0;
241: #endif
242:     if (mode != ZSTD_ps_auto) return mode; /* if requested enabled, but no SIMD, we still will use row matchfinder */
243:     mode = ZSTD_ps_disable;
244:     if (!ZSTD_rowMatchFinderSupported(cParams->strategy)) return mode;
245:     if (kHasSIMD128) {
246:         if (cParams->windowLog > 14) mode = ZSTD_ps_enable;
247:     } else {
248:         if (cParams->windowLog > 17) mode = ZSTD_ps_enable;
249:     }
250:     return mode;
251: }

300: /* Returns 1 if compression parameters are such that CDict hashtable and chaintable indices are tagged.
301:  * If so, the tags need to be removed in ZSTD_resetCCtx_byCopyingCDict. */
302: static int ZSTD_CDictIndicesAreTagged(const ZSTD_compressionParameters* const cParams) {
303:     return cParams->strategy == ZSTD_fast || cParams->strategy == ZSTD_dfast;
